For over 40 years, “Mac vs. PC” has been technology’s most iconic rivalry. Yet in many ways, it’s been an indirect one. articles.title: YouTube expands AI deepfake detection to politicians, government officials, and journalists
articles.summary: YouTube's AI deepfake detection tool is becoming available to politicians, journalists, and officials, letting them flag unauthorized likenesses for removal.

articles.title: RBI issues new rules on how banks can distribute dividends, links payouts to capital strength
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued stricter prudential norms for bank dividend payouts and profit remittances. The framework links payouts to capital strength, asset quality, and regulatory compliance to ensure financial stability and adequate capital buffers across banks.

articles.title: Investors turn to India as private market outpaces regional peers, McKinsey survey shows
articles.summary: India is emerging as the top pick in ​Asia‑Pacific private markets, offering global ​investors scale and resilience as activity in the Asia-Pacific slows, ​a survey by McKinsey & Company and the Indian Venture and Alternate Capital Association showed.

articles.title: Swedish legaltech Legora hits $5 billion valuation as investors pile money into European AI startups
articles.summary: The announcement comes on the back of a bumper start to the week for European AI companies.

articles.title: From Suez to Hormuz: parallels in imperial overreach
articles.summary:
Seventy years after the 1956 Suez Crisis humbled Britain and France, the world is witnessing a strikingly similar drama at another strategic maritime chokepoint: the Strait of Hormuz. In both cases, a Western power intervened militarily to secure control over a vital artery of global energy — only to face political and strategic humiliation that […]
The post From Suez to Hormuz: parallels in imperial overreach appeared first on Asia Times.

articles.title: Motilal Oswal Wealth launches bond trading platform to widen investors' access to fixed income
articles.summary: Motilal Oswal Financial Services' wealth management arm on Tuesday launched a digital bonds trading platform to enable investors to access fixed income securities.

articles.title: US stocks today: US stocks subdued as investors monitor Mideast tensions
articles.summary: U.S. stock markets and oil prices are holding steady Tuesday after significant volatility since the war with Iran began. President Trump's comments initially raised hopes for a swift end, causing oil prices to plunge, but later remarks introduced uncertainty. Iran's continued attacks maintain pressure on the Middle East, leaving markets awaiting further signals.

articles.title: Kevin Mandia sold his cybersecurity company to Google in 2022. He has a fresh $190 million for a new venture
articles.summary: Mandiant founder Kevin Mandia is staying in cybersecurity after selling his prior company to Google, but now he's focused on AI.

articles.title: How the Iran war and rising energy prices are threatening semiconductor demand
articles.summary: A prolonged U.S.-Israel war against Iran could lead to shortages of key chipmaking materials and higher energy costs which could hurt semiconductor demand.

articles.title: Adobe is debuting an AI assistant for Photoshop
articles.summary: Adobe is also adding new AI-powered image-editing features to Firefly.

articles.title: Oracle earnings will show whether its expensive AI bet is starting to pay off
articles.summary: Oracle's $300 billion deal with OpenAI established it as a major player in AI cloud computing alongside Amazon, Google and Microsoft.

articles.title: Stock market quote of the day by Thomas Rowe Price Jr. | "Change is the investor’s only certainty"
articles.summary: Embracing change is the investor's only certainty, as markets are living systems constantly evolving.  Seeking permanence leads to risk, while adaptability through learning, diversification, long-term thinking, and emotional discipline is key.  Periods of transformation, though uncertain, create significant wealth-building opportunities for those who can navigate them.

articles.title: Top 10 high growth large-cap stocks: Profit surges over 50% YoY in Q3FY26
articles.summary: Several NSE large-cap companies reported sharp YoY profit growth in Q3FY26. Adani Enterprises led with a 9,630% jump, followed by Tata Steel and Indian Oil. Steel, energy, finance, FMCG and hospitality firms recorded profit increases above 50%.

articles.title: US Stocks: Bill Ackman files for combined IPOs of Pershing Square, new fund
articles.summary: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square filed for U.S. ​initial public offerings of his hedge fund and ​a new fund on Tuesday, looking set to join a small club of publicly traded ​alternative asset managers.

articles.title: Volkswagen Group profits take big hit on Porsche shift
articles.summary: Strategic readjustment at Porsche, as well as dwindling sales in both main export markets, the US and China took a big bite out of VW's 2025 profits — which almost halved.

articles.title: Trump bought Netflix and Warner Bros bonds at height of bidding war with Paramount
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump bought more than $1.1 million of Netflix bonds over the last three ​months as the streaming giant unsuccessfully fought Paramount ​Skydance to buy Warner Bros Discovery, according to government disclosures.

articles.title: State Bank of India to tap infrastructure bonds after a 16-month hiatus: Bankers
articles.summary: India's largest ​lender, State Bank of ​India, will issue infrastructure bonds ​in March after a gap of about 16 months, two bankers said on Tuesday.

articles.title: US Stocks Today | US stocks futures rise on hopes for early end to Middle East conflict
articles.summary: U.S. stock index futures ticked up on Tuesday, ​as investors hoped for a quicker resolution to the Middle East conflict that has led to a spike in energy prices and stoked inflation concerns, following President Donald Trump's comments on the war.

articles.title: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC among 5 stocks at 52-week high; rally up to 20% in a month
articles.summary: The benchmark Sensex surged 639 points to close at 78,205 on Tuesday. During this rally, five BSE 500 stocks reached their 52-week highs, signaling strong upward momentum and potential for further growth. These stocks, including Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC and JB Chemicals, have shown significant gains in the past month.

articles.title: Volkswagen to cut 50,000 jobs as profits drop
articles.summary: Europe's largest carmaker said post-tax profits had dropped to their lowest level since 2016.

articles.title: Puravankara inks JV to develop housing project in Bengaluru with Rs 1,300cr revenue potential
articles.summary: Realty firm Puravankara Ltd has formed a joint venture to develop a housing project in Bengaluru with an estimated revenue of Rs 1,300 crore.

articles.title: Gold ETF inflows collapse 78% in one month; investors finally rotating back to equity: Shweta Rajani
articles.summary: Gold ETF inflows have seen a significant drop, but experts say it is a natural settling after a rally.  SIP collections are stable, unaffected by market swings.  Investors are showing more sophistication, opting for flexicap funds over pure large-cap options.  This indicates growing resilience among Indian retail investors navigating market turbulence.

articles.title: Rupee shored up by retreating oil prices, likely cenbank intervention
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee strengthened on Tuesday, recovering from a historic low. Falling oil prices and intervention by the Reserve Bank of India supported the currency. Global markets also saw gains following predictions of a swift end to the Middle East conflict. Energy prices remain a key factor influencing currency markets.

articles.title: NSE tells brokers to disclose and remit excess STT retained for FY24 and earlier years
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange has instructed its members to report and pay any extra Securities Transaction Tax collected. This applies to tax collected for the financial year 2023-24 and previous years. The directive follows a request from the Income Tax Department. Members must submit details and remit excess tax with interest.

articles.title: February SIP dip is a calendar glitch, March numbers will be much higher, says SBI MF's DP Singh
articles.summary: February's dip in SIP collections was attributed to fewer processing days, not a retail investor retreat. SBI Mutual Fund's DP Singh stated that SIP closures remain low, with market volatility favoring rupee-cost averaging. He also highlighted that gross flows, not net inflows, better reflect fresh retail participation, and expects stable inflow patterns ahead.

articles.title: Uber rolls out women-only option in the US
articles.summary: The feature allows both women riders and drivers to be matched with other women for trips on the ride-sharing app.

articles.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX weighs Nasdaq listing after seeking early index entry
articles.summary: Elon Musk's rocket and satellite maker SpaceX is leaning toward listing its shares on the Nasdaq ​for what could rank as the biggest initial public offering of all time, according to four people familiar with the company's thinking.

articles.title: Currency, tax risks tempering private equity optimism in India, investors say
articles.summary: Currency depreciation and high ​taxes remain key constraints on ​private equity (PE) returns from India, investors say, even as the ​country's strong growth outlook and improving exit environment cement its place in the emerging market space.

articles.title: County group to invest over Rs 1,200 cr to build luxury housing project in Noida
articles.summary: Realty firm County Group will invest more than Rs 1,200 crore to develop a luxury housing project in Noida.

articles.title: Singapore bourse to offer bond futures for India, Southeast Asia
articles.summary: Singapore Exchange is set to introduce futures contracts for government bonds from India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, and the Philippines. This move aims to provide investors with a tool to manage risks associated with interest rate fluctuations. The new products will be available in three, five, and ten-year maturities.

articles.title: MFs pull back: Stakes cut in 11 smallcap stocks in Q3 after two quarters of buying
articles.summary: Mutual funds have sold shares in 67 BSE small-cap companies. This move follows two quarters of buying. Many of these stocks have seen their prices drop significantly this fiscal year. Some have gained substantially. However, the focus is on the major losers. This shift in mutual fund strategy warrants investor attention.

articles.title: Explained: Why gas prices are rising in India amid Iran war
articles.summary: Gas prices are rising in India as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and halted LNG production in Qatar squeeze global supplies. With India heavily dependent on imported LPG and LNG, the crisis is pushing up domestic cooking gas prices and triggering shortages across major cities and key industries.

articles.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei ends higher as traders gauge efforts to calm energy
articles.summary: Japan's ​Nikkei share gauge closed higher ​on Tuesday, recovering some of the losses ​from the previous session, as investors reacted to moves by major economies to tap oil reserves to stabilise markets shaken by the Middle East ‌conflict.

articles.title: Global Markets | European shares rebound on hopes of Middle East de-escalation
articles.summary: European shares bounced back on Tuesday as investor sentiment was lifted after U.S. President Donald Trump said the war ‌in Middle ⁠East ⁠could come to a quick end.

articles.title: Surging hedging costs show rising angst for India’s stock market
articles.summary: Hedging costs in India have surged to their highest levels since 2024, with the India VIX jumping far above realised volatility as traders brace for further turmoil. The Nifty 50 has mirrored global weakness sparked by the Iran war, signalling rising anxiety across India’s $4.8-trillion equity market.

articles.title: Pharma and PSU banks emerge as safe havens as markets navigate volatility
articles.summary: Indian equities showed stability mid-session Tuesday, with the Nifty holding above key levels and Bank Nifty outperforming. Easing oil prices and declining volatility boosted investor sentiment, though global uncertainties persist. Analysts suggest a selective approach, favoring defensive sectors like pharma and PSU banks for potential opportunities.

articles.title: Happiest Minds shares rocket 12% as firm raises guidance to 12.5% on AI First launch success
articles.summary: Happiest Minds Technologies' shares surged over 12.5% after the company revised its FY27 growth expectation to 12.5%, up from 10%. This upward revision is attributed to the rapid adoption of its AI First strategy and other initiatives, which are generating traction ahead of prior expectations.

articles.title: India gains favour with global investors on returns, ESG momentum
articles.summary: India ​is maturing as a market ​in which foreign investors who take into account environmental, ​social and governance (ESG) factors are confident in dedicating more of their portfolio to, according to fund managers at the Indian Venture and Alternate Capital Association conclave ‌in Mumbai, who ⁠cite ⁠the economy's scale, improving return outlook and advancing ESG standards as key differentiators.

articles.title: Iran war: How long before Gulf nations stop pumping oil?
articles.summary: The oil price spiked near $120 after strikes on Iran's energy sites and the shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. With tankers stuck and Gulf oil facilities hit, producers are running short on storage to maintain production.

articles.title: Correction in banks offers opportunity; private lenders may lead next rally: Digant Haria
articles.summary: Market expert Digant Haria suggests the banking stock correction is a cooling-off phase, not due to deteriorating fundamentals. He anticipates private sector lenders will regain investor favor as valuations become attractive, especially with foreign institutional selling potentially stabilizing, paving the way for significant rallies.

articles.title: Gas crisis hits India amid Middle East war: These 30 stocks likely to see biggest impact
articles.summary: Indian cities are experiencing gas supply shortages due to the ongoing Middle East conflict, leading to price surges for domestic and commercial LPG. Several sectors, including fertilizers, restaurants, and tile manufacturers, are directly impacted as supply constraints worsen. The government has issued new regulations to prioritize essential gas supplies amidst the crisis.
